### Fix 3
Line: 127
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: ./details-item
  parentName: DetailsItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  126            {canUpdate ? (
  127              <Button
  128                data-test="edit-annotations"

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/details-page/details-page.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix, then read the file to apply them.

**Fix 1**: `Button variant="link"` - The type changed to add `'stateful'`, but `"link"` is still a valid value. No code change needed.

**Fix 2**: `useAccessReview` signature changed. Need to read the file to see how it's used and determine if any changes are needed.

**Fix 3**: Icon children in `<Button>` must be moved to the `icon` prop. Need to see the actual JSX around line 127.

Let me read the file:

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Line 132, `variant="link"`)**: The type changed to *add* `'stateful'` as a new valid value. The existing value `"link"` is still valid in the new type. No code change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 13, `useAccessReview`)**: The signature of `useAccessReview` changed. Looking at line 58, the call is:
```typescript
const canUpdateAccess = useAccessReview({...});
```
The signature change shows the parameters are the same shape (resourceAttributes object + optional impersonate). The usage here passes a single object argument. The truncated signatures suggest the change is in return type or other parameters not affected by this usage. No code change is needed for how it's called here.

**Fix 3 (Line 127, Button with icon)**: The Button around lines 127-138 contains both text (`{t('{{count}} annotation', ...)}`) AND an icon (`<PencilAltIcon .../>`). Per the fix instructions, I need to move the icon to the `icon` prop and keep the text as children.

Let me look at the current Button code more carefully:

Lines 127-138:
```tsx
<Button
  data-test="edit-annotations"
  type="button"
  isInline
  onClick={annotationsModalLauncher}
  variant="link"
>
  {t('{{count}} annotation', {
    count: _.size(metadata.annotations),
  })}
  <PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />
</Button>
```

This has text AND an icon. Per Fix 3 instructions: move the icon to the `icon` prop, keep the text as children.

arguments.after:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              type="button"
              isInline
              onClick={annotationsModalLauncher}
              variant="link"
              icon={<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />}
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', {
                count: _.size(metadata.annotations),
              })}
            </Button>
arguments.before:
<Button
              data-test="edit-annotations"
              type="button"
              isInline
              onClick={annotationsModalLauncher}
              variant="link"
            >
              {t('{{count}} annotation', {
                count: _.size(metadata.annotations),
              })}
              <PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />
            </Button>

content.text: Let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. All imports are still used (`PencilAltIcon` is now referenced in the `icon` prop on line 133), and the structure is clean.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** (Line 132) | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union. The type change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option — no existing values were removed. |
| **Fix 2** (Line 13) | `useAccessReview` signature changed | **No change needed** | The call site at line 58 passes a single `AccessReviewResourceAttributes` object. The signature change (truncated in the rule) does not affect this usage pattern — the parameter shape remains compatible. |
| **Fix 3** (Line 127) | Icon children → `icon` prop on `<Button>` | **Applied** | Moved `<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />` from Button children to the `icon` prop (line 133). The text content `{t('{{count}} annotation', ...)}` remains as children. The `PencilAltIcon` import on line 6 is still referenced. |

chunks.response:
The file looks correct. All imports are still used (`PencilAltIcon` is now referenced in the `icon` prop on line 133), and the structure is clean.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** (Line 132) | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union. The type change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option — no existing values were removed. |
| **Fix 2** (Line 13) | `useAccessReview` signature changed | **No change needed** | The call site at line 58 passes a single `AccessReviewResourceAttributes` object. The signature change (truncated in the rule) does not affect this usage pattern — the parameter shape remains compatible. |
| **Fix 3** (Line 127) | Icon children → `icon` prop on `<Button>` | **Applied** | Moved `<PencilAltIcon className="co-icon-space-l pf-v6-c-button-icon--plain" />` from Button children to the `icon` prop (line 133). The text content `{t('{{count}} annotation', ...)}` remains as children. The `PencilAltIcon` import on line 6 is still referenced. |
